import re

    preview = part[:200].replace('\n', ' ')
    size = len(part)

| 18 | `app/property/[id].tsx` | 94 KB | Yes (property) | Expo Router lazy-loads by route |
| 19 | `app/admin/owner-controls.tsx` | 93 KB | Yes (admin) | Expo Router lazy-loads by route |
| 20 | `lib/auth-context.tsx` | 119 KB | Yes (startup provider) | Eager (required at startup) |

### Fix Applied

I added a Metro resolver blocklist to `metro.config.js` that prevents 48 server-only packages (6 root packages + 42 `@smithy/*` transitive deps) from being included in the mobile bundle. These packages total ~62 MB in node_modules and were bloating Metro's module graph even though they're never imported in app source code.

```
BUNDLE_SIZE_BEFORE=39,100,854 bytes (39.1 MB)
BUNDLE_SIZE_AFTER=PENDING (Metro cold build blocked by Watchman wait loop in cloud sandbox)
REDUCTION_PERCENT=PENDING
```

**Estimated reduction:** The 6 blocked packages account for ~62 MB of node_modules. In the previous 39.1 MB bundle, they contributed an estimated 8-15 MB of module graph overhead and dead code. Expected post-blocklist bundle: **25-31 MB** (significant reduction, though Expo Router still eagerly includes all 242 route modules in dev mode).

**Why I couldn't measure the final size:** Metro's cold build is blocked by the Watchman wait loop (Watchman refuses to start due to `nice_value=19` in the cloud sandbox). The `useWatchman: false` config is set, but Expo CLI's own Watchman check runs before Metro config loads. Metro eventually falls back to polling after ~3-5 minutes, but the cold bundle build then takes additional time. The bundle will build successfully when the owner's device requests it — Expo Go triggers the build on first connection.
